title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins' power play struggled post-Olympics, ranking 28th in the NHL with a 12.8 percent success rate.
Third line forwards Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm have produced zero points in 11 games since Olympic break.
Defensive pairing issues with Andrew Peeke and Nikita Zadorov, being outscored 4-2 in recent 5-on-5 play.

title: 5 Red Sox takeaways as Wilyer Abreu and Eduardo Rodriguez lift Venezuela to World Baseball Classic title
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Venezuela won its first World Baseball Classic title, defeating Team USA 3-2, with Wilyer Abreu hitting a key home run.
Former Red Sox pitcher Eduardo Rodriguez contributed 4.1 scoreless innings, while current player Brayan Bello excelled for the Dominican Republic.
Jarren Duran and Masataka Yoshida had strong performances for their respective teams, contributing offensively during the tournament.

title: This Ashland mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children with complex medical needs and their parents
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mengyuan Flanagan from Ashland is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Michael Lisnow Respite Center.
The center supports children with complex medical needs and provides relief for their families.
Flanagan, a volunteer at the center, finds running improves her mental health and is inspired by marathon runners.

title: Western Mass. couple found guilty of manslaughter in death of foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker convicted of manslaughter in death of 10-month-old foster child in Adams, Massachusetts.
The child died from treatable complications as prosecutors argued the couple ignored clear signs he needed medical attention.
The couple faces up to 20 years for manslaughter and will be sentenced on March 19 according to the DA's office.

title: Major water main break in Jackson Square floods streets, causes detours
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A major water main broke in Jackson Square Tuesday, flooding streets and disrupting traffic at Columbus, Centre, and Ritchie.
Boston Police rerouted traffic, and MBTA closed Jackson Square busway, affecting routes 14, 22, 29, 41, and 44 buses.
Crews worked to shut off a 36-inch transmission main; daycare businesses evacuated and streets remained flooded by evening.

title: Why Jaylen Waddle’s trade to Broncos is bad news for the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Waddle traded to Broncos for three draft picks, including a 2026 first-rounder.
Trade strengthens Broncos, creating tougher competition for Patriots in AFC.
Patriots face challenges trading for A.J. Brown due to high market value for receivers.

title: 2 charged after car hits pedestrians outside of downtown Boston nightclub
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Darius Boyce, 45, and Maria Moraes, 25, charged after Jeep hit three pedestrians outside Boston's ICON nightclub.
Boyce faces assault charges plus firearm and reckless driving violations; Moraes charged with interfering with police.
Incident involved vehicle fleeing and stolen items; suspects identified via license plate, stopped, and arrested by police.

title: Former President Biden makes surprise St. Patrick’s Day appearance in Boston
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former President Joe Biden made an unscheduled appearance at Boston's St. Patrick’s Day Breakfast in the Seaport District.
Biden spoke about immigrant experiences, comparing his Irish ancestors' journey to current challenges under President Trump's policies.
Governor Maura Healey joined Biden, emphasizing Boston's connection to Irish heritage and current immigration issues.

title: 2 Boston College students injured in deck collapse during off-campus party
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Boston College students fell from a second floor deck in Brighton, sustaining injuries during an off-campus party.
The incident involved a deck railing snapping off; police noted rotting wood that was painted over to conceal damage.
The injured students were taken to local hospitals, and a city inspector assessed the remaining structural safety.

title: Patriots rookie earns hefty performance bonus after strong 2025 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Craig Woodson led the Patriots' defense with 950 snaps in 2025, starting 15 games as a rookie.
Woodson earned a performance bonus of $1,149,910, ranking 10th in NFL payout distribution for 2025.
In the playoffs, Woodson totaled 30 tackles and six pass breakups, excelling in Super Bowl LX.
